name: managing-branches
description: Git branching strategy expertise with flow-aware automation. Auto-invokes when branching strategies (gitflow, github flow, trunk-based), branch creation, branch naming, merging workflows, release branches, hotfixes, environment branches, or worktrees are mentioned. Integrates with existing commit, issue, and PR workflows.
capabilities: Create and validate branches following Gitflow, GitHub Flow, trunk-based, or GitLab Flow strategies
Generate branch names with proper prefixes, issue references, and naming conventions
Automate gitflow operations including feature start/finish, release preparation, and hotfix workflows
Manage git worktrees for parallel development without stashing
Validate branch names, flow compliance, and merge targets against configured rules
Clean up merged branches and worktrees with safety checks
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: Legitimate Git branching management skill. All static findings are false positives: path traversal alerts are git worktree paths, weak crypto alerts are SHA-1 for content hashing, and backtick alerts are documentation examples. Subprocess calls use hardcoded git command arrays with no user input injection risk. No network access, no credential handling, no persistence mechanisms.
valueStatement: Managing Git branches manually leads to inconsistent naming, merge conflicts, and release chaos. This skill provides flow-aware automation for branch creation, validation, and cleanup across Gitflow, GitHub Flow, trunk-based, and GitLab Flow strategies.
userTitle: Manage Git branches with automated workflows
limitations: Requires Git 2.17+ for full worktree support
Configuration file must exist for advanced features to work properly
Cannot modify remote repository branch protection rules directly
Does not handle merge conflicts automatically - requires manual resolution

useCases.title: Standardize team branching
useCases.description: Enforce consistent branch naming, prefixes, and merge workflows across the entire team with automated validation.

useCases.title: Automate release workflows
useCases.description: Streamline release branch creation, version bumping, changelog generation, and multi-branch merging.

useCases.title: Parallel feature development
useCases.description: Use git worktrees to work on multiple branches simultaneously without losing context or stashing changes.

promptTemplates.title: Create feature branch
promptTemplates.prompt: Start a feature branch for user authentication based on issue 42

promptTemplates.title: Finish feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Finish the current feature branch and merge it following gitflow conventions

promptTemplates.title: Emergency hotfix
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hotfix branch from main for security vulnerability with isolated worktree

promptTemplates.title: Validate branch policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate all branches in this repository follow our naming conventions and flow rules

bestPractices: Initialize the branching configuration before first use to define your strategy and naming rules
Use worktrees for parallel development to avoid context switching and stashing changes
Validate branch names before creation to catch naming violations early
Clean up merged branches regularly to keep the repository organized
antiPatterns: Creating branches directly on main without going through the configured flow
Using underscores or uppercase letters in branch names (breaks convention)
Keeping feature branches alive longer than necessary (causes merge conflicts)
Merging to the wrong target branch (feature to main instead of develop)

faq.question: Which branching strategies are supported?
faq.answer: Gitflow, GitHub Flow, GitLab Flow, trunk-based development, and simple flow templates are included.

faq.question: How long can feature branches live?
faq.answer: For gitflow: 1-2 weeks max. For trunk-based: less than 2 days. Shorter branches reduce merge conflicts.

faq.question: Does this integrate with GitHub?
faq.answer: It works with local git operations. GitHub integration requires additional GitHub CLI or API skills.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: All operations run locally. Configuration is stored in your project directory. No external network calls are made.

faq.question: Why are my branches failing validation?
faq.answer: Check branch naming rules in your config. Common issues: uppercase letters, underscores, missing prefixes, or wrong base branch.

faq.question: Can I use this with any Git hosting service?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with local git operations and is compatible with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and self-hosted git servers.
